content:
## Hermes Agent by Nous Research ‚Äî Complete Research Findings

### 1. GitHub Repo URL & Latest Version
- **Repo:** https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
- **Latest version:** **v0.16.0** (as of June 2026)
- **License:** MIT
- Recent "Surface Release" (June 2026) introduced a native desktop app for macOS/Linux/Windows with one-click install, in-app self-updates, drag-and-drop files, integrated model picker

---

### 2. Installation Method (Linux/WSL)
```bash
curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash
```
**Windows native (PowerShell):**
```powershell
iex (irm https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.ps1)
```

**What the installer does:**
- Installs dependencies: Python 3.11, Node.js, `uv`, `ripgrep`, `ffmpeg`
- Clones the repository
- Sets up virtual environment and global `hermes` command
- Configures LLM provider settings

**Post-install:**
1. Reload shell: `source ~/.bashrc` or `source ~/.zshrc`
2. Run setup wizard: `hermes setup` (or `hermes setup --portal` for Nous Portal OAuth flow)
3. Start chatting: `hermes`

---

### 3. Configuration System
**Location:** `~/.hermes/`

**Files:**
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `config.yaml` | Primary config ‚Äî model, terminal, display, memory, toolsets |
| `.env` | Secrets ‚Äî API keys, tokens, passwords |
| `auth.json` | OAuth provider credentials (e.g., Nous Portal) |

**Format:** YAML (config.yaml)

**Example `config.yaml` structure:**
```yaml
model:
  default: "anthropic/claude-opus-4.6"
  provider: "auto"
  base_url: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"

agent:
  max_turns: 90
  reasoning_effort: "medium"

terminal:
  backend: "local"  # options: local, ssh, docker, singularity, modal, daytona

display:
  skin: "default"
  compact: false
  show_reasoning: false

memory:
  memory_enabled: true
  user_profile_enabled: true

toolsets:
  - all
```

**Precedence (highest first):**
1. CLI arguments (e.g., `hermes chat --model ...`)
2. `~/.hermes/config.yaml`
3. `~/.hermes/.env`
4. Built-in defaults

**Config CLI commands:**
- `hermes config view` ‚Äî view current config
- `hermes config edit` ‚Äî open in editor
- `hermes config set KEY VAL` ‚Äî set a value (auto-routes to correct file)
- `hermes config migrate` ‚Äî update config schema for new versions
- Supports `${VAR_NAME}` env variable interpolation in YAML

---

### 4. Skills System
**Location:** `~/.hermes/skills/`

**Directory structure:**
```
my-skill/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ SKILL.md        # Required: Main instructions and metadata
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ scripts/        # Optional: Helper scripts (.py, .sh)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ references/     # Optional: Background materials/documentation
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ templates/      # Optional: Output format templates
```

**SKILL.md format ‚Äî YAML frontmatter + markdown instructions:**
```yaml
---
name: my-skill
description: A brief description of what this skill does
version: 1.0.0
author: Your Name
license: MIT
platforms: [macos, linux]
metadata:
  hermes:
    tags: [Category, Subcategory]
    related_skills: [other-skill-name]
    requires_tools: [web_search]
---

# Instructions
Explain how the agent should handle the task...
```

**Creating custom skills:**
- **Manual:** Create directory + SKILL.md in `~/.hermes/skills/`
- **Auto-generation:** Agent automatically writes SKILL.md after solving complex tasks (self-improving loop)
- Compatible with `agentskills.io` open standard
- Uses "progressive disclosure" pattern for token optimization

**40+ built-in skills included.**

**CLI commands:**
- `hermes skills search <query>` ‚Äî find community skills
- `hermes skills install <skill-path>` ‚Äî install skills
- `/skills browse` ‚Äî in-session slash command to browse skills hub

**When to use Skill vs Tool:**
- **Skill:** Instructions, shell commands, wrapping CLI/API tools ‚Äî no custom Python needed
- **Tool:** Custom Python logic, binary/streaming data, precise end-to-end integration

---

### 5. Model Provider Configuration

**OpenRouter (first-class support):**
```bash
# Interactive
hermes model  # select OpenRouter from list

# Manual
# ~/.hermes/.env
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...

# ~/.hermes/config.yaml
model:
  provider: openrouter
  default: anthropic/claude-sonnet-latest
```

**Ollama (local models):**
```bash
# Automated
ollama launch hermes   # auto-detects Ollama, configures endpoint

# Manual
hermes model  # select "Custom endpoint"
# Enter base URL: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
```

**Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint:**
Set `base_url` in config.yaml to any gateway/proxy (Portkey, local Ollama, etc.) and provide API key in `.env`.

**Important:** Requires model with **‚â•64,000 token context window** ‚Äî agent may reject models below this.

---

### 6. Terminal Backend (Docker Sandbox)

**Options:** `local`, `ssh`, `docker`, `singularity`, `modal`, `daytona`

**Docker backend:**
- Commands run inside a persistent Docker container
- "Long-lived" ‚Äî changes to working directory, installed packages, env tweaks persist across tool calls, subagents, and sessions
- NOT a fresh container per command

**Configure:**
```bash
hermes config set terminal.backend docker
```

---

### 7. Gateway System (Telegram/Discord/etc.)

**Supported platforms:** Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal + CLI

**Setup:**
```bash
hermes gateway setup   # interactive wizard
hermes gateway         # start the gateway service
```

**Telegram setup:**
1. Create bot via @BotFather ‚Üí `/newbot` ‚Üí get API Token
2. `hermes setup` ‚Üí enter Telegram Bot Token
3. Optionally set Telegram User ID for access restriction
4. Check `/setprivacy` in BotFather for group chats

**Discord setup:**
1. Discord Developer Portal ‚Üí New Application ‚Üí Bot tab ‚Üí copy Token
2. Enable "Message Content Intent"
3. Use OAuth2 URL generator to invite bot to server
4. `hermes setup` ‚Üí enter Discord token and channel IDs
5. Config option: `group_sessions_per_user: false` for shared channel transcript

**Designed to run on VPS/server for 24/7 operation.**

---

### 8. Web Dashboard

**Yes, it has a web dashboard!**

```bash
hermes dashboard
```

- **URL:** `http://127.0.0.1:9119`
- **Features:**
  - Profile Builder (guided flow for identity, models, skills, MCP servers)
  - Settings/API key management
  - Active session monitoring
  - Embedded "Chat" tab with full TUI (slash commands + streaming tool output)
- **Security:** Binds to loopback `127.0.0.1` by default

---

### 9. Memory System

**Layered architecture:**

| Component | Description | Limit |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| `MEMORY.md` | Agent's personal notes ‚Äî environment, project facts, learned info | 2,200 chars |
| `USER.md` | Info about user ‚Äî communication style, preferences, expectations | 1,375 chars |

- Both files injected into system prompt as "frozen snapshot" at session start
- **Session Search:** SQLite + FTS5 for searching past conversation transcripts
- **External Memory Providers:** Honcho, Mem0, Hindsight, etc. via `hermes memory setup` (one active at a time, injected into user message)

---

### 10. Tools System

**~70 built-in tools organized into toolsets:**

| Category | Tools |
|----------|-------|
| Web Search & Extraction | `web_search`, `web_extract` (Firecrawl backend) |
| Browser Automation | `browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_snapshot`, `browser_console`, `browser_vision` |
| File & Terminal | `read_file`, `patch`, `terminal`, `process` |
| Agent Orchestration | `todo`, `clarify`, `execute_code`, `delegate_task`, `memory`, `session_search`, `cronjob` |
| Media & Multimodal | `vision_analyze`, `image_generate`, `video_generate`, `text_to_speech` |

**Nous Tool Gateway:** Bundled subscription for web search (Firecrawl), image gen (FLUX), browser automation (Browser Use), TTS ‚Äî all behind single Nous subscription, enabled via `hermes setup --portal`.

**MCP (Model Context Protocol) Integration:**
- Dynamic tool loading from external MCP servers
- Tools appear with prefix: `mcp_github_create_issue`
- Commands: `hermes mcp list`, `hermes mcp add`, `hermes mcp configure`
- Supports `stdio` (local) and `HTTP` (remote) transports

**View tools:** `hermes tools` or `/tools` in-session

---

### 11. CLI Commands (Full List)

**Top-level commands:**
| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `hermes` / `hermes chat` | Start interactive chat |
| `hermes chat -q "prompt"` | Run single non-interactive query |
| `hermes setup` | Initial setup wizard |
| `hermes setup --portal` | Setup via Nous Portal (OAuth) |
| `hermes model` | Select/switch model provider |
| `hermes gateway` | Start messaging gateway |
| `hermes gateway setup` | Configure gateway platforms |
| `hermes dashboard` | Launch web dashboard (port 9119) |
| `hermes profile` | Manage profiles (`list`, `use`, `create`, `delete`, `show`) |
| `hermes config view/edit/set/migrate` | Configuration management |
| `hermes auth` | Manage credentials (add, list, remove, reset) |
| `hermes status` | Show agent/auth/platform status |
| `hermes cron` | Inspect/trigger cron scheduler |
| `hermes tools` | List available tools |
| `hermes skills search/install` | Manage skills |
| `hermes mcp list/add/configure` | Manage MCP servers |
| `hermes memory setup` | Configure external memory providers |
| `hermes doctor` | Health check / diagnostics |
| `hermes update` | Pull latest code + reinstall deps |
| `hermes uninstall` | Remove (use `--full` for complete cleanup) |

**Global flags:**
- `--tui` / `-t` ‚Äî full-screen TUI
- `--profile <name>` / `-p` ‚Äî specific profile
- `--version` / `-V` ‚Äî version info
- `--resume <session_id>` / `-r` ‚Äî resume specific session
- `--continue` / `-c` ‚Äî resume most recent session
- `--verbose` ‚Äî debug output

**In-session slash commands:**
- `/help`, `/model`, `/tools`, `/skills browse`

---

### 12. Known Issues with WSL2 / Windows

**Permission/filesystem problems:**
- Fix: Add metadata options to `/etc/wsl.conf`:
```ini
[automount]
options = "metadata,umask=22,fmask=11"
```
- Then `wsl --shutdown` in PowerShell

**Systemd/service errors (gateway won't start):**
- Check: `ps -p 1 -o comm=` ‚Äî if not `systemd`, add to `/etc/wsl.conf`:
```ini
[boot]
systemd=true
```

**Audio/TTS issues:** TTS may not route to Windows speakers from WSL2 ‚Äî check GitHub issues for community workarounds (PowerShell audio fallbacks).

**"Another instance is running" false positives:** Use `hermes update --force` if certain no other instances are active.

**Diagnostic:** Run `hermes doctor` for health check.

**Alternative:** Native Windows PowerShell install avoids WSL2 complexity entirely.

---

### Community & Real-World Notes
- Subreddit: r/hermesagent (weekly "Friday check-in" threads with configs)
- Users recommend starting with ONE workflow, not automating everything at once
- Use **multiple profiles** to segment tasks (coding, research, personal)
- Common use cases: research automation, inbox triage, code review, personal automation
- Generally seen as better for background/autonomous tasks vs Claude Code for interactive coding
- Desktop app is newer and may feel restrictive for power users preferring Docker/VPS setups
